title: phaseolus vulgaris var. oblongus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. oblongus, commonly known as the kidney bean, is a variant of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) characterized by its elongated shape and is native to the Americas. The kidney bean contains phytohemagglutinin (PHA), a lectin that has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of kidney beans include their high protein content, fiber, and low glycemic index, making them a nutritious addition to a balanced diet, and they have been linked to reduced risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Kidney beans can be prepared in various ways, including cooking them as a side dish, adding them to soups and stews, and using them in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures.
